candidates.why: This is a COGS-related D&A component, but it appears to roll into the selected parent row 'Cost of goods sold' via the visible 'Cost of goods sold - Balancing value' plus D&A components, so adding it separately would double count.
This is a COGS-related D&A component, but it appears to be included within the selected 'Cost of goods sold' total, as indicated by the balancing structure shown in the excerpt.

variables.notes: Selected COGS reference bucket is 'Cost of goods sold'. The excerpt shows that 'Cost of goods sold' equals the balancing value plus 'Amortization in COR/COGS' and 'Depreciation in COR/COGS', so those D&A rows are already embedded in the selected COGS row and should not be added separately.

candidates.why: This D&A row appears to be a component of the selected 'Selling and administrative costs' row, because the selected row matches the sum of this item, 'Depreciation in selling expense', and 'Other Administrative'. Adding it separately would double count relative to the chosen XSGA bucket.
This appears to be another component embedded in the selected 'Selling and administrative costs' row rather than a separate same-level add-on.

Although operating and D&A-related, this belongs to the separate 'Administrative expenses' branch, not clearly to the already selected XSGA reference bucket 'Selling and administrative costs'. Adding only this D&A without the corresponding parent admin expense would be inconsistent and risks distortion.

variables.notes: Relative to the selected XSGA bucket 'Selling and administrative costs', the visible selling-expense D&A rows look embedded in that selected row. 'Depreciation in administrative expense' appears tied to a separate 'Administrative expenses' row that was not selected in the earlier mapping, so it should not be added here.
